category.name: Charts
category.preferred_workflow: Use native Aspose.Words chart APIs directly. Create or load local sample DOCX inputs inside the example whenever the task references an existing file, stream, folder, table, or multi-document batch. Insert or locate a real chart shape, then modify Chart, ChartSeries, ChartDataLabel, ChartAxis, ChartLegend, and related objects through documented APIs only.

strict_api_bans: Do not modify a shape as if it were a chart unless shape.HasChart is true.
Do not invent chart APIs, enums, or formatting members that are not part of Aspose.Words.
Do not remove or add series without validating current series availability and compatible data structure.
canonical_patterns: Insert charts with DocumentBuilder.InsertChart using the required ChartType.
Bootstrap local DOCX inputs when a task mentions an existing file, paragraph, chart, stream, or folder.
Validate series and point counts explicitly for verification scenarios.
Balance table insertion state before inserting a chart into a cell.
nullable_warning_prevention: Initialize all non-nullable reference type properties to avoid CS8618 warnings.
Avoid CS8600, CS8602, and CS8604 by guarding maybe-null values before dereference or assignment.
Declare nullable locals when a value may legitimately be null, then null-check before use.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Code treats a generic Shape as a chart without checking HasChart.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Validate shape.HasChart before accessing the Chart property.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Category count and series values do not match expected chart structure.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Clear default data if needed and add aligned categories and series values deterministically.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Chart insertion in a table cell fails because the builder is left in an unbalanced table state.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Balance StartTable, InsertCell, EndRow, and EndTable before saving.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Existing DOCX, folder, or stream scenarios assume inputs already exist.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Create local sample input documents or folders inside the example first.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: CS8600, CS8602, or CS8604 around maybe-null chart shapes or located nodes.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use nullable locals and guard maybe-null values before dereference.

examples.task: Load an existing DOCX file, locate a chart shape by its title, and replace its data source.
Insert a column chart into a new document using DocumentBuilder.InsertChart with default data.
Add a scatter chart to an existing paragraph by calling DocumentBuilder.InsertChart with the appropriate overload.
Insert a chart using a two‑dimensional array as a custom data source, mapping series and categories.
Insert a chart into a table cell and ensure it scales proportionally with the cell dimensions.
Clone a chart shape from one document section and insert the duplicate into another paragraph.
Retrieve the Shape.Chart object from an inserted chart and modify its title text programmatically.
Create a new chart series, set its values via the series.Values property, and assign custom category labels.
Use ChartSeriesCollection.Add overload accepting a name and values to create a labeled series in one step.
Add multiple ChartDataPoint objects to a series and set each point's color using the Fill property.
Change the chart type from column to line after populating data to demonstrate dynamic transformation.
Add a trendline to a scatter chart series and configure its type, color, and display equation.
Configure ChartDataLabel number format to display percentages with one decimal place for all series data points.
Align multi‑line chart data labels to the center and enable text wrapping for better readability.
Define default ChartDataLabel options to apply consistent font size and color across all chart series.
Customize the chart's data label font to use a specific typeface, size, and bold styling for emphasis.
Enable data label leader lines for a pie chart and customize their length for better placement.
Configure the chart to display data labels only for points exceeding a specified threshold value.
Set major gridlines visibility on the primary X‑axis and customize their line color and thickness.
Adjust the primary Y‑axis scaling to fixed minimum and maximum values and set major unit intervals.
Set display units for the secondary X‑axis to thousands and format axis labels with a custom number format.
Set the secondary Y‑axis number format to currency with two decimal places for financial charts.
Apply a solid fill color to the chart plot area and add a gradient overlay for visual depth.
Programmatically set the chart's background fill to a semi‑transparent color to create a watermark effect.
Add a border stroke to the chart legend with specified thickness and dash style for emphasis.
Adjust the chart legend position to the top right corner and set its background fill to light gray.
Update the chart title text and toggle legend visibility based on user preferences.
Apply a predefined chart style template to a newly inserted chart to ensure consistent visual branding.
Apply a three‑dimensional rotation effect to a column chart to enhance visual perspective.
Programmatically change the chart's plot area border to a dashed line with specific color and width.
Programmatically hide the chart's plot area border while keeping axis lines visible for a clean appearance.
Enable automatic resizing of chart elements when the document page size changes to maintain layout integrity.
Retrieve existing chart series, modify their data points, and refresh the chart display.
Remove a specific series from a chart using ChartSeriesCollection.RemoveAt with the correct index.
Validate that a chart contains the expected number of series and data points before saving.
Validate that all chart series have matching category counts to prevent data misalignment errors during rendering.
Export a Word document containing multiple charts to PDF while preserving chart formatting and data labels.
Implement error handling to catch exceptions when inserting a chart into a read‑only document stream.
Batch process a folder of Word files, adding a predefined bar chart to each document's first page.
